title: Taco Bell Business Stats Taco Bell shows strong business momentum across scale, sales, digital de

slides.speaker_note: This slide summarizes Taco Bell’s business momentum across footprint, sales, and growth. The key takeaway is that the brand combines large U.S. scale with significant system-wide sales and positive same-store sales growth, indicating continued consumer demand and operational strength.

ui.description: A clean two column layout with a narrative summary area on the left and stacked metric callouts on the right.

components.description: Right side vertical metric column with three repeated numeric callouts and small colored label markers.

summary_bullet_list: Taco Bell demonstrates **strong scale** with thousands of U.S. restaurants reported for 2026.
System-wide sales highlight **major brand demand** and broad market reach across the business.
Latest full-year same-store sales growth signals **continued traffic and sales strength**.

content.__speaker_note__: This slide summarizes Taco Bell’s business momentum across footprint, sales, and growth. The key takeaway is that the brand combines large U.S. scale with significant system-wide sales and positive same-store sales growth, indicating continued consumer demand and operational strength.

slides.speaker_note: This slide summarizes Taco Bell’s U.S. restaurant footprint as of April 27, 2026. The key takeaway is that the network is broad, with 8,238 restaurants nationwide, but major states drive a meaningful share. California is the largest state presence, followed by Texas and Florida.

ui.description: A clean two column slide with a left-aligned text section and a large supporting image on the right.

components.description: Full slide background layer providing a plain white canvas for the complete composition.
Left column component containing the main heading, accent divider, and descriptive body copy.

runs.text: Focus on companies with 500+ employees in Financial Services, Healthcare, and Technology sectors. Target $3.5M in new pipeline with sub-$150 CAC through account-based marketing and content-led strategies.

components.description: Right column component with a large rectangular image aligned slightly above the slide center.

left_content_block.body_paragraph: As of April 27, 2026, Taco Bell operates **8,238 U.S. restaurants**. California leads with **883 locations**, about **11%** of the network, followed by Texas at **9%** and Florida at **6%**.

supporting_image.image_prompt: Clean business infographic style image of a United States map highlighting California, Texas, and Florida with Taco Bell-inspired purple and bell accents, restaurant location markers, modern minimal background, no text.

content.__speaker_note__: This slide summarizes Taco Bell’s U.S. restaurant footprint as of April 27, 2026. The key takeaway is that the network is broad, with 8,238 restaurants nationwide, but major states drive a meaningful share. California is the largest state presence, followed by Texas and Florida.

slides.speaker_note: This slide highlights the brand’s scale across three major states. California leads with 11% of U.S. locations, totaling 883 restaurants. Texas follows with 9%, or about one restaurant per 39,397 people. Florida represents 6%, with about one restaurant per 44,012 people.

ui.description: A clean centered title layout with a small accent rule and three evenly spaced metric summary columns below.

components.description: Full-slide neutral background layer supporting the centered foreground content without visual distraction.
Centered headline area with a short decorative accent line positioned beneath the title.
Three horizontally arranged metric groups with large values, supporting labels, and small performance captions.

content.__speaker_note__: This slide highlights the brand’s scale across three major states. California leads with 11% of U.S. locations, totaling 883 restaurants. Texas follows with 9%, or about one restaurant per 39,397 people. Florida represents 6%, with about one restaurant per 44,012 people.

slides.speaker_note: This slide summarizes Taco Bell’s 2022 sales performance. System-wide sales reached 13.5 billion dollars, supported by average unit volume of 2.1 million dollars per restaurant. U.S. sales per capita were reported at 40 dollars annually, indicating broad market penetration and strong restaurant-level productivity.

ui.description: A clean dashboard slide with a prominent title, bordered chart panel on the left, and stacked metric summaries on the right.

components.description: Full slide background component providing a clean white canvas for the dashboard layout.
Large headline area in the upper left with a short colored accent rule underneath.
Large left chart area with a subtle bordered panel containing a two-series line chart visualization.
Right column of four evenly spaced metric summaries, each with a large value and compact label row.

content.__speaker_note__: This slide summarizes Taco Bell’s 2022 sales performance. System-wide sales reached 13.5 billion dollars, supported by average unit volume of 2.1 million dollars per restaurant. U.S. sales per capita were reported at 40 dollars annually, indicating broad market penetration and strong restaurant-level productivity.

slides.speaker_note: This slide highlights two key economic drivers: digital ordering and franchising. Digital sales reached 2 billion dollars in 2022, showing strong customer adoption. At the same time, a 93 percent franchised restaurant base supports growth with lower direct operating requirements.

ui.description: A split presentation layout with narrative content on the left and a vertical stack of metric cards on the right.

components.description: Static full slide background with white content area and pale side panel for visual separation.
Large title, accent rule, bold objective statement, and body paragraph arranged as a left-aligned narrative block.

runs.text:
Accelerate enterprise customer acquisition across
EMEA and North America

Focus on companies with 500+ employees in Financial Services, Healthcare, and Technology sectors. Target $3.5M in new pipeline with sub-$150 CAC through account-based marketing and content-led strategies.

components.description: A right-side column of three KPI cards, each with a colored header, target details, and gauge visual.

left_narrative_block.objective_statement: Show how **digital adoption** and franchising strengthen scalable growth.
left_narrative_block.supporting_paragraph: Digital ordering reached **$2B in 2022 sales**, reflecting strong customer adoption. With **93% franchised restaurants**, the model supports expansion while reducing direct operating requirements.

content.__speaker_note__: This slide highlights two key economic drivers: digital ordering and franchising. Digital sales reached 2 billion dollars in 2022, showing strong customer adoption. At the same time, a 93 percent franchised restaurant base supports growth with lower direct operating requirements.

slides.speaker_note: Taco Bell’s latest reported full year showed 7% same-store sales growth. In Q4 2025, both overall visits and same-store visits increased year over year, supported by promotions, menu innovation, National Taco Day activity, and the return of Cheesy Dipping Burritos.

ui.description: Two-column dashboard slide with a summary metric on the left and a staged horizontal funnel visualization on the right.

components.description: Full-slide neutral background component that establishes the clean white canvas for the dashboard.
Large top-left heading with a short accent line beneath it for visual hierarchy.
Left-side key performance indicator with a large percentage and short two-line caption.
Right-side staged funnel made of connected labels, proportional horizontal bars, and embedded conversion details.

content.__speaker_note__: Taco Bell’s latest reported full year showed 7% same-store sales growth. In Q4 2025, both overall visits and same-store visits increased year over year, supported by promotions, menu innovation, National Taco Day activity, and the return of Cheesy Dipping Burritos.

slides.speaker_note: Taco Bell shows sustained demand across income bands, with especially strong traction among consumers ages 18 to 24. The reported annual volume of 2 billion tacos reinforces the brand’s broad product appeal and continued relevance with a wide customer base.

ui.description: A centered title with accent divider sits above a large bordered card containing a donut chart visualization.

components.description: Full slide white background layer for the overall presentation canvas.
Centered heading area with a short accent divider directly beneath the title.
Large bordered content card with a single centered donut chart and built-in legend and labels.

content.__speaker_note__: Taco Bell shows sustained demand across income bands, with especially strong traction among consumers ages 18 to 24. The reported annual volume of 2 billion tacos reinforces the brand’s broad product appeal and continued relevance with a wide customer base.

slides.speaker_note: This slide summarizes Taco Bell’s core business strengths. The brand benefits from broad U.S. scale, a highly franchised model, productive restaurants, and expanding digital sales. Its recent momentum is also tied to effective promotions, ongoing menu innovation, and strong relevance with younger quick-service restaurant customers.

summary_bullet_list: **Large U.S. scale** and strong franchise penetration support Taco Bell’s resilient business profile.
**High sales productivity** and growing digital revenue reinforce operating momentum.
**Consistent traffic gains** are supported by promotions, menu innovation, and younger QSR appeal.
